name: ZCCM Investments Holdings PLC
description: ZCCM Investments Holdings PLC is a premier Zambian mining investment holding company, listed on the LuSE Main Board (1995), with secondary listings on Euronext Access Paris and the London Stock Exchange. The company holds equity stakes in multiple copper and other mineral mining operations across Zambia, including through joint ventures with global mining companies. ZCCM-IH operates through two segments: Investments (equity stakes in mines) and Mining & Processing (direct operations including gold, amethyst, manganese, limestone, and coal). The company also provides environmental consultancy, engineering, and energy services.

financials.netIncomeNote: Includes substantial fair value gains, disposal gains, and share of profit from associates — non-recurring in nature. Normalized/recurring earnings are significantly lower.

eps.note: Headline EPS includes one-off gains; normalized EPS estimated at ZMW 6-7 based on market PE

ratios.peNote: Based on headline EPS of ZMW 247.80 the PE would be ~0.17x. The reported 6.5x PE suggests the market is using normalized/core earnings of approximately ZMW 6.46 per share. DATA RECONCILIATION NEEDED.

topHolders.name: Government of Zambia (GRZ)
topHolders.note: Majority shareholder via IDC; exact stake needs confirmation

keyInvestments.name: Mopani Copper Mines PLC
keyInvestments.note: Historical majority stake; partial divestment completed

keyInvestments.name: Other mining & energy assets
keyInvestments.note: Multiple smaller equity stakes in Zambian mining operations

analystNotes.note: ZCCM-IH is the hardest LuSE company to value. The FY2024 reported net profit of ZMW 39.85 billion on revenue of just ZMW 3.21 billion screams 'non-recurring items.' This is typical for mining investment holding companies where fair value gains, disposal proceeds, and share of associate profits can dwarf operating revenue. The market appears to price ZCCM-IH on normalized/core earnings (~PE 6.5x), not headline EPS of ZMW 247.80 (which would imply a PE of 0.17x — absurd). Total assets of ZMW 57.29 billion against a market cap of ~ZMW 9.5 billion means the stock trades at ~0.17x book — either deeply undervalued or the assets carry significant liabilities and contingent obligations. DEEP-DIVE FORENSIC ANALYSIS RECOMMENDED.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskFactors: Extreme earnings volatility — headline profits driven by non-recurring items
Complex corporate structure with multiple subsidiaries and associates
Government majority ownership — political and policy risk
Opaque financial reporting — difficult to separate recurring from one-off earnings
Mining sector cyclicality — copper price dependency
Market pricing disconnect — trades at ~0.17x book value
investmentThesis: ZCCM-IH is a 'too hard' pile for most investors. It's a government-controlled mining investment vehicle where headline earnings are meaningless and normalized earnings are anyone's guess. Trading at 0.17x book value, it's either the cheapest mining stock in the world or a value trap where the assets are encumbered by liabilities, social obligations, and government interference. Until a clear reconciliation between headline EPS (ZMW 247.80) and the PE the market is actually using (6.5x) is completed, no credible investment thesis can be formed.
stance: AVOID — NEEDS FORENSIC DEEP DIVE BEFORE ANY POSITION
dataSources: ZCCM-IH 2024 Integrated Annual Report (via AfricanFinancials, Scribd)
ZCCM-IH FY2024 Abridged Audited Financial Results — LuSE SENS (Jun 2025)
dataQuality: MEDIUM — Core financials from audited 2024 annual report are confirmed, but EPS/PE reconciliation between headline and market-implied earnings requires further investigation. Ownership breakdown needs verification.
